visual_checks: Identify all prerecorded video content that includes audio (speech, meaningful sound effects)
Check if the video player shows a closed caption (CC) toggle button
If captions are visible, verify they match what is being spoken and include relevant non-speech sounds
Look for caption quality: proper punctuation, speaker identification, timing synchronization
Check if captions are positioned so they do not obscure critical visual content
pass_conditions: All prerecorded synchronized media has captions available
Captions accurately represent all dialogue and narration
Captions identify speakers when it is not visually obvious who is speaking
Meaningful sound effects are captioned (e.g., [applause], [phone ringing])
Captions are synchronized with the audio — appearing when spoken and disappearing appropriately
fail_conditions: Prerecorded video with audio has no captions available
Captions are present but significantly inaccurate (wrong words, missing large sections)
Captions do not identify different speakers in multi-speaker content
Important non-speech audio (sound effects, music conveying meaning) is not captioned
Captions are present but completely out of sync with the audio
na_conditions: Page contains no prerecorded video with audio content
All video content is video-only (no audio track)
Media is clearly labeled as a media alternative for text and identified as such
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Video could not be played to verify caption presence
Cannot determine if the video is prerecorded or live
common_mistakes: Auto-generated captions (YouTube auto-captions) with no human review — often full of errors
Captions that only include dialogue but miss important sound effects like alarms or music cues
Captions that do not distinguish between speakers
Open captions burned into the video that obscure visual content and cannot be turned off
Captions that paraphrase instead of accurately transcribing dialogue

examples.pass: A training video has synchronized closed captions that identify each speaker by name, include [background music] and [door slams], and accurately transcribe all dialogue
examples.fail: A product demo video has speech throughout but no caption option in the player and no transcript provided
examples.partial: Captions are available but appear to be auto-generated with noticeable errors in technical terminology and no speaker identification

plain_meaning: When prerecorded video contains audio that carries information (dialogue, narration, meaningful sound), captions must be provided that present the spoken content and relevant non-speech sounds synchronized with the video. The intent is that deaf and hard-of-hearing users get the same information hearing users get. Auto-generated captions from a platform count only when they are accurate; captions that are gibberish or wildly wrong do not satisfy the rule. The criterion does not apply when the media is itself a media alternative for text and is clearly labeled as such.
scope_applies_to: Prerecorded synchronized media on the page (video with an audio track)
Embedded third-party players (YouTube, Vimeo, Wistia, etc.) that host prerecorded video with audio
Self-hosted <video> elements with an audio track
Video carousels, hero-video backgrounds if they carry meaningful audio content
Linked video files offered as page content
scope_does_not_apply_to: Live synchronized media -- that belongs to SC 1.2.4
Prerecorded audio-only content (podcasts) -- that belongs to SC 1.2.1
Prerecorded video-only (silent) content -- that belongs to SC 1.2.1
Audio description of visual content -- that belongs to SC 1.2.3 / 1.2.5
Media that is a media alternative for text on the same page and is clearly labeled as such
Keyboard access to the video player -- that belongs to SC 2.1.1
Missing accessible name on the video's containing iframe or player button -- that belongs to SC 1.1.1 / 4.1.2
Contrast of caption text rendering -- that belongs to SC 1.4.3
Videos with purely decorative ambient audio carrying no information (rare; verify before exempting)
borderline_calibration: Auto-generated captions count as captions IF they are accurate. Obviously garbled auto-captions are a fail.
Captions must cover dialogue AND important non-speech sounds (e.g. '[applause]', '[door slams]') when those are meaningful.
Presence of a CC button in the player UI is evidence but not proof -- if you can verify captions play, that's sufficient.
If the video has no audible content at all, 1.2.2 is not applicable; route the concern to 1.2.1 for video-only.
Missing captions on informational prerecorded video is a hard failure at medium+.
